mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in real GDP signal a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and increases consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds rate and rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4% and a 2-month decline in average hourly earnings signal a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate and declining average hourly earnings are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month rise in the yield curve signal a weakening supply chain and increasing uncertainty that reduces corporate profitability and increases risk aversion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production and rising yield curve are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.2 million and a 2-month rise in consumer spending signal a strengthening housing market and increasing consumer confidence that increases corporate profitability and reduces risk aversion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts and rising consumer spending are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
